title: Transportation Issue Graph — the Reader/Writer contract
description: The ONLY channel between the Transportation Reader (CBI: observes, diagnoses, never edits) and the Transportation Writer (Token2Net: edits, never observes the world directly). Readers emit issue OBJECTS, not prose; the Planner (human + AI) flips status to approved; Writers act only on approved issues. Reader can never write. Writer can never read the world. The Planner stays in the loop — the AI never loops itself.

reader_role.description: asserts the emitter is a Reader — Writers must refuse graphs without this

world_read.description: what the reader observed: gmns_network, probe_speed, trajectory, counts, od, signals, osm, imagery, simulation_result

type.description: machine vocabulary, e.g. missing_turn, missing_ramp, disconnected_path, recurring_bottleneck, incident_pattern_day, capacity_drop, fd_fit_implausible, data_quality, model_misses_onset

location.description: the network/world objects involved — the graph edges

evidence.description: machine-checkable observations, e.g. 'movement missing', 'path disconnected', 'qc_pass_rate 0.94', 'episode P_min 125'

recommended_token.description: SUGGESTED writer actions (from the writer's token schema) — suggestions only; the Planner disposes

status.description: lifecycle: only the Planner may set approved/rejected; only the Writer may set patched; only validation may set validated

review.description: the Planner gate — REQUIRED before any write

rendering.description: OPTIONAL human-facing prose generated FROM the structure (planner_message, public_message). Never the contract — a Writer must ignore this field entirely.
